rationale: New multi-turn agentic safety benchmark shifts eval practice for teams deploying agents: measures cumulative harmful action drift across turns, directly relevant to agentic compliance and model-risk governance in FS.

Audits 11 long-context benchmarks and 4 flagship model releases, finding systematic positional blind spots in reasoning evals—directly changes how practitioners should benchmark long-context agents and RAG pipelines, including agentic FS workflows.

New hierarchical FS benchmark fills a real gap in LLM eval for finance (beyond FinQA/TAT-QA), giving DS teams a concrete tool to assess model competence across risk, compliance, and investment domains.

Benchmark for temporal LLM failure modes in statutory QA (post-cutoff staleness, recency bias) is directly relevant to FS teams deploying LLMs for regulatory/compliance research under evolving rules like SR 11-7 or MiFID.

ActInv shows split inference leaks private data via intermediate activations—directly relevant to banks deploying LLMs on-prem or in hybrid edge/server setups under data-residency and SR 11-7 constraints.
Step-by-step Node.js repo building MCP from raw JSON-RPC through a local GGUF agent loop is directly clonable by engineers wanting to run tool-use agents on-prem without cloud API dependencies.

New framework decomposing benchmark gaming into environment vs. model components directly challenges how teams validate frontier models, shifting eval design practice and raising governance questions for any org relying on benchmark results.

HalluScan benchmarks 72 configs across 6 detection methods and 4 model families — directly usable by DS teams evaluating hallucination risk in LLM pipelines, with clear FS relevance for model risk and RAG validation.
Fine-tuning LLMs for KYC name-matching across scripts/transliterations is a load-bearing Tier-2/Tier-1 hybrid directly addressing a named FS pain point, but no code/repo is confirmed available yet.

Head-to-head embedding benchmark (GE2 vs BGE-M3/E5/LaBSE) with chunking ablation and CPU latency data gives RAG practitioners concrete model-selection signal, with multilingual angle relevant to FS cross-border document retrieval.

Addresses a real scaling bottleneck in agentic systems—context-window exhaustion from enumerating skills—by proposing retrieval-augmented skill selection; practical if paper ships code, but v2 repost limits freshness.

Training-free memory retrieval framework for long-horizon agents addresses a real context-window cost/accuracy tradeoff practitioners building agentic workflows face today, with FS relevance for compliance and ops agents.

Unified cost-performance framework for LLM context management (RAG vs compression tradeoffs) is practically relevant for DS/engineers deploying long-context LLMs, but no code/repo confirmed yet — technique-level signal, not drop-in utility.

New benchmark exposing that LLM-based GPU kernel agents overfit sandboxes and fail production inference stacks — directly relevant to engineers building or evaluating agentic code-gen for inference optimization.

llama.cpp PR fixes KV-cache checkpoint corruption that forces full 70k-token context reprocessing in agentic coding loops — directly actionable for engineers running local LLM inference with tools like opencode.

OpenSkillEval offers a concrete eval framework for auditing LLM agent skills/workflows—directly useful for engineers selecting or building skills for agent pipelines, but no FS-specific angle and no code/repo confirmed yet.

Empirical finding that VLMs pass vision benchmarks without actually using visual tokens is a real eval-validity signal practitioners deploying multimodal models need to account for in their test suites.

First systematic cross-lingual red-team study across GPT-5/Claude/Qwen/Pixtral reveals language+modality shift attack surface—directly actionable for teams deploying MLLMs in multilingual or multimodal contexts.

Phase-aware inference controller routing single-GPU LLM requests across FP16/GPTQ/INT8/speculative-decoding modes is a practical latency-cost technique for engineers serving Llama-class models on constrained hardware, but no code release confirmed.

KG-R1 uses RL to collapse multi-module KG-RAG pipelines into a single agentic policy, cutting inference cost and schema dependency — directly relevant to FS teams using KGs for fraud/AML or verifiable reasoning, but no public repo confirmed yet.
ProxyCoT addresses a real gap—LLMs failing on long-context reasoning despite 10M-token windows—with a training framework, but no public code/weights yet limits immediate adoption.

Proposes a personalized agentic RL framework addressing heterogeneous user preferences and tool-use decisions — directionally important for agent builders, but no code/repo yet limits immediate adoption.
ARES automates rubric construction for RL fine-tuning of LLMs—directly relevant to teams doing RLHF/RLAIF at scale—but no code/repo confirmed yet, limiting immediate drop-in utility.

Proposes probability-based SFT objectives to replace NLL for post-training LLMs, addressing real generalization limits — actionable for DS teams fine-tuning models, but no code/repo confirmed and v3 suggests incremental not breakthrough.

Claim-level RAG eval benchmark for legal text is directly relevant to FS teams building RAG over contracts/regulations, but no code/repo confirmed and legal-domain scope limits general practitioner uptake.
ROCm-native LLM inference engine for AMD RDNA3 GPUs with no PyTorch dependency is directly usable by engineers running local Qwen 3.6 MoE, but niche hardware scope limits broader signal.

MAS-Orchestra proposes holistic LLM-driven orchestration + controlled benchmarks to diagnose when MAS beats single-agent — useful framing for engineers building multi-agent pipelines, but no released code/repo confirmed yet.

Entropy-phase-transition framing for when CoT helps vs. hurts is a genuine Tier-1 insight on inference cost/quality tradeoffs, but no code/benchmark released yet limits immediate practitioner adoption.
SkillOpt proposes a disciplined text-space optimizer for agent skills—relevant to agentic AI practitioners, but single-paper with no released code yet limits immediate adoptability.
New benchmark for LLM agent memory architectures beyond simple fact retrieval — useful for DS/engineers designing agentic memory systems, but no code/repo confirmed and limited FS-specific angle.

GT-HarmBench fills a real gap—multi-agent safety evals across 1,535 game-theoretic scenarios—but no code/API is surfaced yet, limiting immediate practitioner uptake; relevant for leaders planning agentic deployments.
Red-teaming framework for LLM 'Overton Windows' on influence ops is Tier-1 (agent evals/failure modes) hitting tomorrow+practical signals, but weak FS angle and no repo/code confirmed yet.

QASC addresses a real RAG pain point (fixed chunking's precision-recall tradeoff) with a query-aware approach, but no code/repo is visible yet, limiting immediate adoption for DS teams building RAG pipelines in FS.

Enterprise-scale real-time risk event extraction from noisy incident streams is a genuine FS ops pain (SRE/AML adjacency), but this is a v3 arXiv paper with no public repo or API, limiting immediate adoptability.

Challenges the strong-teacher assumption in LLM pretraining distillation with empirical results, relevant to teams training smaller models, but no code/repo yet and limited immediate practitioner action beyond awareness.

Interesting Tier-1 research finding—representational convergence doesn't imply reasoning convergence across 16 LLMs—but no code/benchmark release yet and limited immediate practitioner action beyond model-ensemble skepticism.
